events.title: Schueberfouer (Funfair)
events.areaNote: Kirchberg and the old town are a quick bus/tram ride away; Limpertsberg is lively in the evening during big events at Glacis.
events.gettingThere: Tram: get off near Glacis (city center/Kirchberg line). It’s also walkable from the city center in about 20–25 minutes; buses also stop around the fairgrounds.
events.goodToKnow: Go after dark for the best atmosphere. Weekends are busiest; bring cash/card for food stalls and rides.
events.location: Champ du Glacis (Parking Glacis), Luxembourg City
events.description: Luxembourg’s biggest annual funfair: rides, games, street food (including famous Gromperekichelcher), and a festive atmosphere in the city. Traditionally ends with discounted attractions and fireworks.

events.title: National Day celebrations
events.areaNote: This is the historic upper town—expect pedestrian streets, viewpoints over the Pétrusse valley, and the main squares for ceremonies.
events.gettingThere: Best by public transport (bus/tram) to the city center, then walk—many streets close for crowds. Arrive early for good viewing spots.
events.goodToKnow: Expect large crowds and street closures. Keep an eye on the official schedule and arrive early for parade viewpoints.
events.location: Luxembourg City (Place de la Constitution and city center)
events.description: One of Luxembourg’s biggest traditions. Festivities begin on the eve (22 June) with a changing of the guard and a torchlight procession, plus concerts and an open-air party. On 23 June: official ceremony, military parade, and Te Deum at the cathedral.

events.title: Winterlights (Christmas markets & winter festival)
events.areaNote: Most markets are in the walkable center (Ville Haute). It’s easy to move between squares on foot, and views over the valleys are close by.
events.gettingThere: Bus/tram to the city center, then walk between the main squares. Evenings get busy—public transport is easier than driving.
events.goodToKnow: Go on weekdays for shorter lines. Dress warm—wind can be strong on open squares.
events.description: Winter festival with Christmas markets across the city, lights, concerts, exhibitions, and workshops for kids—designed for all ages and a festive atmosphere.

events.title: Éimaischen (Easter Monday crafts market)
events.areaNote: The Old Town is the historic core with narrow streets and small squares—perfect for markets, short walks, and viewpoints.
events.gettingThere: Bus to the city center/old town and walk. Streets can be crowded; wear comfortable shoes for cobblestones and hills.
events.goodToKnow: Arrive early if you want the best selection of Péckvillercher (limited, unique each year).
events.location: Old Town streets (around Marché aux Poissons, Rue du Marché aux Herbes, etc.)
events.description: Traditional crafts market (at least since 1827), famous for pottery and the small bird-shaped terracotta whistles called Péckvillercher—sold only at Éimaischen and different every year.

events.title: Fréijoer op der Gëlle Fra (spring programme)
events.areaNote: This is one of the city’s classic viewpoints over the Pétrusse valley—very central and easy to combine with a walk through the old town.
events.gettingThere: Bus/tram to the city center, then a short walk to Place de la Constitution. Good spot for photos—especially near sunset.
events.goodToKnow: Best enjoyed in late afternoon when the viewpoint is golden-hour friendly; check weather for the best views.
events.location: Place de la Constitution (Gëlle Fra), Luxembourg City
events.description: Spring entertainment programme with floral décor, seating, food stalls, and activities. Includes attractions like a panoramic tower and family-friendly rides.

places.name: Bock Casemates
places.areaNote: Old Town (Ville Haute + lower Grund nearby) is the UNESCO historic core—best for walking, viewpoints, and short scenic routes.
places.gettingThere: Walk from the city center (Ville Haute). From Grund, you can go up via the Pfaffenthal lift + paths. Nearby bus stops are in/around the old town; parking is limited.
places.goodToKnow: Bring a light jacket: it can feel cool inside. Allow 45–90 minutes depending on your route and photo stops.
places.description: Underground tunnels carved into the rock as part of Luxembourg’s fortifications (UNESCO-listed old quarters and fortifications). Great for history + cool views over Grund and the Alzette valley.

places.name: Grand Ducal Palace (exterior)
places.areaNote: Right in the pedestrian-friendly historic center—best explored on foot with nearby squares, cafés, and shops.
places.gettingThere: Bus/tram to the city center, then walk. Combine with a short loop past Place Guillaume II and nearby old town streets.
places.goodToKnow: You can usually view the exterior any time; guided visits may be seasonal—check the official site before planning.
places.description: Iconic building in the city center and official residence of the Grand Duke. A key stop when walking through the historic center.

places.name: Cathedral Notre-Dame de Luxembourg
places.areaNote: Central Old Town area with major monuments close together—easy to visit several highlights in one walk.
places.gettingThere: Bus/tram to the city center, then a short walk. It’s close to Place d’Armes and Place Guillaume II.
places.goodToKnow: Respect quiet hours and services. Early morning tends to be calmer for photos.
places.description: Historic cathedral in the center of Luxembourg City, known for its architecture and role in national ceremonies.

places.name: Gëlle Fra (Monument of Remembrance)
places.areaNote: A central viewpoint spot above the Pétrusse valley—great for photos and starting a scenic walk down into the parks below.
places.gettingThere: Bus/tram to the city center, then walk to Place de la Constitution. Nearby routes lead down to the Pétrusse parks.
places.goodToKnow: Great photo stop. Combine with a walk down into the Pétrusse valley parks for a quieter route.
places.address: Place de la Constitution, Luxembourg City
places.description: Famous monument in the city center and a key viewpoint area overlooking the Pétrusse valley.

places.name: Mudam Luxembourg
places.areaNote: Kirchberg is the modern district: wide boulevards, museums, and EU buildings—very different vibe from the old town.
places.gettingThere: Tram to Kirchberg, then a short walk through Park Dräi Eechelen. Easy to combine with Fort Thüngen and nearby viewpoints.
places.goodToKnow: Plan 60–120 minutes. Check the museum site for exhibitions and ticket info before you go.
places.description: Modern and contemporary art museum in Kirchberg (next to Fort Thüngen). Good choice if you want an indoor visit and a very modern part of the city.

places.name: National Museum of History and Art (MNHA)
places.areaNote: Old Town museum area with several sights within a few minutes’ walk—ideal if you want culture + a historic atmosphere.
places.gettingThere: Walk from the city center; it’s near Marché-aux-Poissons. Combine with the Corniche for viewpoints after your visit.
places.goodToKnow: Allow 60–120 minutes. Pair with nearby old town viewpoints for a great half-day plan.
places.description: Major museum in the old town covering archaeology, history, and art collections.

places.name: Parc Municipal
places.areaNote: Green space right next to the center—good for a quiet break without leaving the main sightseeing area.
places.gettingThere: Easy walk from the city center (Avenue Monterey area). Good stop between Ville Haute sights and nearby viewpoints.
places.goodToKnow: Perfect for a short break. Bring snacks/coffee and enjoy 20–40 minutes between sightseeing stops.
places.description: Central park right by the city center—easy walk, benches, and green space. Nice break between sightseeing stops.

places.name: Panoramic Elevator of the Pfaffenthal
places.areaNote: Pfaffenthal is the lower valley neighborhood—quieter, scenic, and connected to the upper city by the panoramic lift.
places.gettingThere: Tram/bus to the city center or Kirchberg, then follow signs to the lift. Great link between upper city walks and valley routes.
places.goodToKnow: Use it as a shortcut between the upper city and valley walks. Great views even if you don’t continue the route.
places.description: Panoramic lift offering impressive views and an easy connection between upper city and Pfaffenthal.

places.name: Chemin de la Corniche
places.areaNote: Cliff-edge viewpoints above the Grund—classic Luxembourg City scenery and one of the best “quick wow” walks.
places.gettingThere: Start from the Old Town (Ville Haute) and follow signs to the Corniche. Easy to combine with Bock Casemates and the Grund below.
places.goodToKnow: Best in clear weather. Sunset hours can be spectacular but busier.
places.address: Chemin de la Corniche, Luxembourg City
places.description: Scenic walkway along the fortifications with famous panoramic views—often called “the most beautiful balcony in Europe”.

places.name: Wenzel Circular Walk (Wenzel Walk)
places.areaNote: A walking route through the UNESCO old quarters—expect stairs, viewpoints, and a mix of upper town and valley paths.
places.gettingThere: Begin in the Old Town (many starting points). Wear good shoes—there are slopes and steps. You can shorten it by skipping some loops.
places.goodToKnow: Plan 60–120 minutes. It’s easier in dry weather because of steps and slopes.
places.description: Popular city walk that takes you through the old quarters and along UNESCO-listed fortifications.

places.name: Place d’Armes cafés
places.areaNote: A classic central square—good people-watching and an easy meet-up spot between sights.
places.gettingThere: Right in the pedestrian center; easiest by bus/tram to the city center and then a short walk.
places.goodToKnow: Try it in the morning for a calm coffee, or early evening for the liveliest atmosphere.
places.description: Popular square with cafés and places to sit and enjoy the city.
